IR-4(12),"IRO-08, IRO-13","Incident Handling | Malicious Code and Forensic Analysis

Analyze malicious code and/or other residual artifacts remaining in the system after the incident.",Contributes,"VCF provides a robust foundation for collecting critical data necessary for post-incident forensic analysis and the identification of residual artifacts.

The platform's comprehensive logging capabilities, including system, audit, and network flow logs from ESXi hosts, vCenter Server, NSX, and vSAN, are aggregated and analyzed through Aria Operations for Logs. NSX further enhances visibility by providing detailed network traffic analysis, firewall logs, and advanced threat prevention features, enabling the identification of network-based indicators of compromise and communication patterns associated with malicious activity. This rich telemetry is essential for understanding the scope and impact of an incident.

The customer is responsible for deploying and integrating specialized malicious code analysis tools, endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions, and forensic analysis toolkits within or alongside the VCF environment. This includes defining the processes for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting residual artifacts and malicious code found within guest operating systems and applications, as well as establishing the procedures for root cause analysis and incident remediation."
IR-4(13),"MON-16, SEA-11, SEA-12","Incident Handling | Behavior Analysis

Analyze anomalous or suspected adversarial behavior in or related to [Assignment: organization-defined environments or resources].",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation provides robust capabilities for analyzing anomalous and suspected adversarial behavior across the entire virtualized infrastructure.

NSX Advanced Threat Prevention (ATP) delivers network detection and response (NDR) functionalities, including advanced threat detection, sandboxing, and intrusion prevention system (IPS) capabilities, to identify and analyze suspicious network traffic and malware. Carbon Black Cloud Workload extends this analysis to the workload level, providing advanced endpoint detection and response (EDR) to monitor process behavior, file system changes, and network connections for indicators of compromise and adversarial tactics. Furthermore, vRealize Log Insight aggregates logs from all VCF components, enabling centralized correlation and analysis of security events and anomalies, while vRealize Operations monitors infrastructure health and performance for unusual patterns that may indicate compromise.

The customer is responsible for defining the specific environments and resources subject to analysis, establishing the organizational policies and procedures for incident handling and behavior analysis, and configuring the VCF security components to align with their specific security posture.
Human security analysts must interpret the findings generated by the platform, conduct deeper investigations into detected anomalies, and initiate appropriate incident response actions."
IR-4(14),OPS-04,"Incident Handling | Security Operations Center

Establish and maintain a security operations center.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation delivers a robust, secure, and observable infrastructure platform that serves as a critical technical foundation for a Security Operations Center. The platform provides comprehensive logging capabilities through Aria Operations for Logs, enabling centralized collection and analysis of events across the virtualized environment. Network security is enforced via NSX Distributed Firewall, offering micro-segmentation and advanced threat prevention, which are essential for a SOC's monitoring and incident response activities. Furthermore, Aria Operations provides extensive performance and health monitoring across the compute, storage, and network layers, supplying vital telemetry for security analysts to detect anomalies and potential threats.

Establishing and maintaining a complete Security Operations Center, however, requires the customer to define and implement organizational policies, operational processes, and dedicated human resources. This includes staffing the SOC with security analysts, engineers, and incident responders, developing incident response playbooks, integrating with external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) and Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response (SOAR) platforms, and managing threat intelligence feeds. VMware Cloud Foundation provides the secure and observable infrastructure but does not encompass the full organizational and operational aspects of a SOC."
IR-4(15),IRO-16,"Incident Handling | Public Relations and Reputation Repair
(a) Manage public relations associated with an incident

(b) Employ measures to repair the reputation of the organization.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation establishes a secure and resilient infrastructure foundation, which inherently reduces the likelihood and impact of technical incidents that could negatively affect an organization's public image. Implement the following cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity and confidentiality of nonlocal maintenance and diagnostic communications: [Assignment: organization-defined cryptographic mechanisms].",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation inherently secures all management and diagnostic communications for its core components, including vCenter Server, ESXi hosts, NSX-T Data Center, and SDDC Manager. column2: prevent] physical tampering or alteration of [Assignment: organization-defined hardware components] within the system.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) integrates with and leverages underlying hardware security features to enhance the integrity of the platform. This capability detects unauthorized modifications to the boot chain, including alterations to firmware or boot media, thereby preventing the loading of malicious or tampered software components.

column1: Furthermore, VCF supports the use of Trusted Platform Modules (TPMs) for cryptographic functions and attestation, establishing a hardware root of trust that can detect unauthorized changes to the system's configuration and boot process. The customer is responsible for defining and implementing organization-defined anti-tamper technologies for physical hardware components beyond the boot chain integrity, such as chassis intrusion detection, physical locks, and environmental controls. Protect the plans from unauthorized disclosure and modification.",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides a fully integrated, software-defined data center infrastructure that inherently defines its constituent system components, including compute, storage, and networking resources. It establishes a clear operational environment, detailing dependencies and connections within the SDDC and to external systems.

The platform implements a comprehensive set of security and privacy controls, such as network segmentation, access controls, encryption, and robust logging, which directly inform the ""controls in place"" section of any system security plan. VCF's architecture supports the technical foundation for security categorization and provides critical data for risk determinations related to the infrastructure's design and configuration.

While VCF provides the secure technical foundation and essential inputs, the customer is solely responsible for the development, documentation, and ongoing management of the system security and privacy plans.

This includes defining the system's operational context, identifying individuals fulfilling system roles, categorizing information types processed by their applications, conducting privacy risk assessments for personally identifiable information, and describing specific organizational threats.

The customer must also establish relevant control baselines, document tailoring decisions, define security and privacy-related activities requiring coordination, and ensure the plans are reviewed and approved by the authorizing official.

Furthermore, the customer is responsible for distributing the plans, defining review frequencies, updating them in response to changes or issues, and protecting the plan documents from unauthorized disclosure and modification."

It offers capabilities such as vSAN encryption and VM encryption, which are critical for protecting data at rest and in transit, thereby contributing to the minimization of privacy risk for personally identifiable information. The platform's architecture is designed for integration into broader enterprise environments, providing APIs and standardized interfaces that facilitate its incorporation into an overarching enterprise architecture.

column1: PS-3(1),HRS-04.1,"Personnel Screening | Classified Information

Verify that individuals accessing a system processing, storing, or transmitting classified information are cleared and indoctrinated to the highest classification level of the information to which they have access on the system.",Contributes,"The VCF platform establishes a secure foundation for systems processing, storing, or transmitting classified information. It provides robust identity and access management capabilities, including integration with enterprise identity providers and granular role-based access controls, to restrict administrative and operational access to the underlying infrastructure components. This ensures that only authenticated and authorized personnel can manage or interact with the platform itself.

The customer is responsible for establishing and maintaining comprehensive personnel security policies, conducting background investigations, granting security clearances, and performing necessary indoctrination for individuals.

The customer must also define and enforce the mapping of these human-level clearances and indoctrination statuses to the technical access roles and permissions within the VCF environment and the applications hosted on it, ensuring that access to classified information is commensurate with an individual's clearance level."
PS-3(2),HRS-04.2,"Personnel Screening | Formal Indoctrination

Verify that individuals accessing a system processing, storing, or transmitting types of classified information that require formal indoctrination, are formally indoctrinated for all the relevant types of information to which they have access on the system.",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PS-3(3),HRS-04.1,"Personnel Screening | Information with Special Protective Measures
Verify that individuals accessing a system processing, storing, or transmitting information requiring special protection:
(a) Have valid access authorizations that are demonstrated by assigned official government duties

(b) Satisfy [Assignment: organization-defined additional personnel screening criteria].",Contributes,"VCF provides the foundational identity and access management framework necessary to enforce access authorizations. It integrates with enterprise identity providers to authenticate users and supports role-based and attribute-based access controls, ensuring that only authorized individuals can access systems processing, storing, or transmitting information requiring special protection. The platform logs all access attempts, providing an auditable record of who accessed what and when.

The organization is responsible for defining and implementing the personnel screening criteria, establishing official government duties, and ensuring that access authorizations are valid and directly tied to those duties. This includes the initial vetting, background checks, and ongoing verification processes that determine an individual's eligibility for access to sensitive information.

VCF enforces the access policies defined by the organization but does not automate the human resources functions of personnel screening or the initial determination of official government duties and corresponding access authorizations."
PS-3(4),HRS-04.3,"Personnel Screening | Citizenship Requirements

Verify that individuals accessing a system processing, storing, or transmitting [Assignment: organization-defined information types] meet [Assignment: organization-defined citizenship requirements].",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PS-4,HRS-09,"Personnel Termination
Upon termination of individual employment:
a. Disable system access within [Assignment: organization-defined time period]
b. Terminate or revoke any authenticators and credentials associated with the individual
c. Conduct exit interviews that include a discussion of [Assignment: organization-defined information security topics]
d. Retrieve all security-related organizational system-related property

e. Retain access to organizational information and systems formerly controlled by terminated individual.",Contributes,"VCF integrates with enterprise identity providers to enforce access controls, ensuring that system access can be disabled and authenticators revoked upon termination. The platform's underlying infrastructure, including vSAN and compute resources, ensures the persistence of organizational information and systems. Role-based access control mechanisms within VCF facilitate the reassignment of access and the establishment of new access pathways to data and systems formerly controlled by terminated individuals, maintaining business continuity.

The customer is responsible for defining the organization-defined time period for disabling access and the specific information security topics for exit interviews. Operational processes for disabling accounts, revoking credentials within the identity provider, conducting exit interviews, and retrieving physical security-related property are entirely customer-managed. Furthermore, the customer must establish and enforce policies for data retention, ownership transfer, and the configuration of application-level access permissions to ensure appropriate access to organizational information and systems post-termination."
PS-4(1),HRS-09.3,"Personnel Termination | Post-employment Requirements
(a) Notify terminated individuals of applicable, legally binding post-employment requirements for the protection of organizational information

(b) Require terminated individuals to sign an acknowledgment of post-employment requirements as part of the organizational termination process.",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PS-4(2),HRS-09.4,"Personnel Termination | Automated Actions
Use [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms] to [Selection (one or more): notify [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] of individual termination actions
column2: disable access to system resources].",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) integrates with enterprise identity and access management (IAM) systems, enabling automated disabling of user access to VCF-managed resources upon personnel termination. This capability ensures that access to vCenter Server, NSX-T Data Center, vSAN, and other SDDC components is automatically revoked. The platform's identity federation capabilities ensure that deprovisioning events in an authoritative identity source are reflected across the VCF environment, thereby disabling access to system resources in an automated fashion.

column1: The organization is responsible for defining the specific automated mechanisms that trigger these actions, including the integration points with HR systems or identity providers. VCF does not inherently provide mechanisms to notify organization-defined personnel or roles of individual termination actions
column2: this aspect typically requires integration with an external HR system, ITSM platform, or a dedicated security orchestration solution to manage and disseminate such notifications."

column1: PS-5,HRS-08,"Personnel Transfer

a. Review and confirm ongoing operational need for current logical and physical access authorizations to systems and facilities when individuals are reassigned or transferred to other positions within the organization
b. Initiate [Assignment: organization-defined transfer or reassignment actions] within [Assignment: organization-defined time period following the formal transfer action]
c. Modify access authorization as needed to correspond with any changes in operational need due to reassignment or transfer

d. Notify [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] within [Assignment: organization-defined time period].",Contributes,"VCF provides robust identity and access management capabilities, leveraging enterprise identity providers for user authentication and authorization across its management and workload domains. This enables granular, role-based access control to VCF components such as vCenter Server, NSX Manager, and vSAN.

The platform facilitates the technical implementation of modifying logical access authorizations to systems and facilities managed by VCF, allowing for adjustments to permissions to align with changes in operational need following personnel reassignments or transfers.

The customer is responsible for establishing the formal policies and procedures governing personnel transfers, including defining the specific transfer or reassignment actions, the required time periods for initiating these actions, and the personnel or roles responsible for notifications. While VCF provides the technical mechanisms to implement access modifications, the determination of ongoing operational need for access, the initiation of transfer workflows, and the management of physical access authorizations require customer-defined processes and human oversight."
PS-6,"HRS-06, HRS-06.1","Access Agreements
a. Develop and document access agreements for organizational systems
b. Review and update the access agreements [Assignment: organization-defined frequency]
c. Verify that individuals requiring access to organizational information and systems:
1. Sign appropriate access agreements prior to being granted access

2. Re-sign access agreements to maintain access to organizational systems when access agreements have been updated or [Assignment: organization-defined frequency].",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) establishes a robust foundation for access control through its integrated identity and access management capabilities. It leverages enterprise identity providers such as Active Directory or LDAP for user authentication and authorization, ensuring that only authenticated and authorized individuals can access organizational systems and information within the VCF environment. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) is enforced across all VCF components, including vCenter Server, NSX-T Data Center, and vSAN, to precisely define and limit user privileges based on their assigned roles. This granular control ensures that access is granted strictly according to defined permissions.

The development, documentation, review, and update of formal access agreements remain an organizational responsibility. VCF does not natively manage the lifecycle of these legal documents, nor does it track the signing or re-signing status of individuals. Organizations must implement external processes and systems to verify that individuals sign appropriate access agreements prior to being granted access to VCF-managed systems and to ensure re-signing occurs when agreements are updated or at defined frequencies. The enforcement of these agreement-related policies is external to the VCF platform's direct technical capabilities."
PS-6(2),"HRS-06, HRS-06.1","Access Agreements | Classified Information Requiring Special Protection
Verify that access to classified information requiring special protection is granted only to individuals who:
(a) Have a valid access authorization that is demonstrated by assigned official government duties
(b) Satisfy associated personnel security criteria

(c) Have read, understood, and signed a nondisclosure agreement.",Contributes,"VCF provides the foundational identity and access management framework necessary to enforce access controls for classified information. The platform integrates with enterprise identity providers to authenticate users and supports granular role-based access control (RBAC) and attribute-based access control (ABAC) to restrict access to specific virtualized resources, data stores, and management interfaces. This ensures that only authenticated and authorized individuals, as defined by external security policies, can interact with sensitive environments. All access attempts and permission changes are logged for audit and accountability purposes.

The verification of valid access authorization based on official government duties, the satisfaction of personnel security criteria, and the confirmation of signed nondisclosure agreements are external human resources and policy management functions. The customer is responsible for establishing and maintaining these personnel security processes, conducting background checks, managing access authorizations, and ensuring all required legal agreements are in place. These external determinations must then be accurately translated into the identity provider and subsequently mapped to VCF's access control policies for technical enforcement."
PS-6(3),HRS-06.2,"Access Agreements | Post-employment Requirements
(a) Notify individuals of applicable, legally binding post-employment requirements for protection of organizational information

(b) Require individuals to sign an acknowledgment of these requirements, if applicable, as part of granting initial access to covered information.",Contributes,"The VMware Cloud Foundation platform establishes a robust framework for managing access to its underlying infrastructure and tenant workloads. It integrates with enterprise identity providers to authenticate users and enforce role-based access controls, ensuring that only authorized individuals can access specific resources. This includes the initial provisioning of access rights to the platform's management plane and tenant environments, which are critical components for protecting organizational information.
However, the platform does not inherently manage the notification of legally binding post-employment requirements or facilitate the signing of acknowledgments for such policies.

These activities are organizational responsibilities, requiring the customer to establish and enforce human resources policies, legal agreements, and associated processes for notifying individuals and obtaining their signed acknowledgment of post-employment obligations.

The customer is responsible for integrating these external processes with their overall access management lifecycle, ensuring that individuals complete all necessary legal and HR requirements before being granted initial access to covered information, including access to the VCF environment itself."
PS-7,HRS-10,"External Personnel Security
a. Establish personnel security requirements, including security roles and responsibilities for external providers
b. Require external providers to comply with personnel security policies and procedures established by the organization
c. Document personnel security requirements

d. Require external providers to notify [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] of any personnel transfers or terminations of external personnel who possess organizational credentials and/or badges, or who have system privileges within [Assignment: organization-defined time period]

e. Monitor provider compliance with personnel security requirements.",Contributes,"The platform provides the robust technical framework for managing and enforcing access controls for external personnel who interact with the environment. It integrates with enterprise identity providers to provision and de-provision access based on defined roles and responsibilities, ensuring that external users are granted only the necessary privileges. Comprehensive audit logs capture all activities performed by external personnel within the platform, enabling detailed monitoring of their technical interactions. This includes capabilities for enforcing multi-factor authentication and session management, directly contributing to the secure handling of external access.

The establishment of overarching personnel security requirements, including specific security roles and responsibilities for external providers, remains a customer-defined policy and contractual obligation. The platform does not manage the requirement for external providers to notify the organization of personnel transfers or terminations
column2: this is an organizational process that must be implemented and enforced by the customer, typically through contractual agreements and integration with identity management workflows. Comprehensive monitoring of provider compliance with all personnel security requirements, extending beyond technical access to the platform, is also a customer responsibility, requiring human oversight and audit processes."

column1: PS-8,HRS-07,"Personnel Sanctions
a. Employ a formal sanctions process for individuals failing to comply with established information security and privacy policies and procedures

b. Notify [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles] within [Assignment: organization-defined time period] when a formal employee sanctions process is initiated, identifying the individual sanctioned and the reason for the sanction.",Contributes,"The platform provides robust security controls, logging, and auditing capabilities that enforce established information security and privacy policies at the infrastructure layer. It ensures that technical configurations align with security baselines and that deviations or unauthorized actions are logged, providing an immutable audit trail.
This technical evidence is critical for identifying instances of non-compliance with security policies and procedures.

Defining and employing a formal sanctions process, establishing the criteria for non-compliance, designating the specific personnel or roles for notification, and determining the appropriate time period for such notifications are organizational responsibilities. The platform does not manage human resources policies, legal frameworks, or the administrative processes involved in initiating, executing, or communicating personnel sanctions."
PS-9,HRS-03,"Position Descriptions
Incorporate security and privacy roles and responsibilities into organizational position descriptions.",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PT-1,"GOV-02, GOV-03, PRI-01, SEA-01","Policy and Procedures
a. Develop, document, and disseminate to [Assignment: organization-defined personnel or roles]:
1. [Selection (one or more): organization-level
column3: system-level] personally identifiable information processing and transparency policy that:

column1: (a) Addresses purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination among organizational entities, and compliance
(b) Is consistent with applicable laws, executive orders, directives, regulations, policies, standards, and guidelines

2. Procedures to facilitate the implementation of the personally identifiable information processing and transparency policy and the associated personally identifiable information processing and transparency controls

b. Designate an [Assignment: organization-defined official] to manage the development, documentation, and dissemination of the personally identifiable information processing and transparency policy and procedures
c. Review and update the current personally identifiable information processing and transparency:
1. Policy [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events]

2. Procedures [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and following [Assignment: organization-defined events].",Contributes,"The platform provides robust document management capabilities for storing personally identifiable information processing and transparency policies and procedures. It ensures secure access control, allowing only authorized personnel to view or modify these critical documents. Version control features track all changes, maintaining a comprehensive audit trail of policy evolution. The platform facilitates the dissemination of these documents to defined organizational personnel or roles through integrated communication channels and secure portals, ensuring broad awareness and accessibility.

The customer is responsible for the actual development and content creation of the personally identifiable information processing and transparency policy, including defining its purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, and ensuring consistency with applicable laws and regulations.

The customer must also develop the specific procedures that facilitate policy implementation. Furthermore, the customer must designate the official responsible for managing these artifacts and define the specific frequency and events that trigger policy and procedure reviews and updates. The actual review and update processes remain a human responsibility."
PT-2,"PRI-04, PRI-04.1, PRI-05.1, PRI-05.4","Authority to Process Personally Identifiable Information
a. Determine and document the [Assignment: organization-defined authority] that permits the [Assignment: organization-defined processing] of personally identifiable information

b. Restrict the [Assignment: organization-defined processing] of personally identifiable information to only that which is authorized.",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides a robust, secure infrastructure that enables the enforcement of restrictions on personally identifiable information (PII) processing. It integrates with enterprise identity management systems to establish role-based access controls, ensuring that only authorized personnel can access or manage systems containing PII. Network segmentation capabilities, powered by NSX-T, allow for granular control over data flows, isolating PII processing environments and restricting communication to only explicitly permitted pathways. Furthermore, VCF supports encryption at various layers, including vSAN data-at-rest encryption and vSphere VM encryption, which can be leveraged to protect PII from unauthorized access or processing.

The determination and documentation of the organization-defined authority and specific types of PII processing remain a customer responsibility. The customer must establish the foundational policies, legal justifications, and business processes that define what constitutes authorized processing. Subsequently, these policies must be translated into specific technical configurations within VCF, such as access control lists, network security policies, and encryption key management, to ensure that processing is strictly limited to the defined and authorized scope."
PT-2(1),DCH-22.2,"Authority to Process Personally Identifiable Information | Data Tagging

Attach data tags containing [Assignment: organization-defined permissible processing] to [Assignment: organization-defined elements of personally identifiable information].",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation provides the secure and scalable infrastructure required to host and manage data classification and tagging solutions. The platform supports the deployment of specialized data loss prevention (DLP) and data governance tools within virtual machines or containers, enabling them to scan and apply tags to data at rest or in transit across the VCF environment. This includes leveraging storage policies and network segmentation capabilities to enforce controls based on data classification.

The direct identification of organization-defined elements of personally identifiable information and the attachment of specific data tags containing organization-defined permissible processing rules are functions performed by specialized data classification applications.

The customer is responsible for selecting, deploying, and configuring these applications within the VCF environment, as well as defining the specific PII elements, permissible processing rules, and the mechanisms for applying these tags."
PT-2(2),PRI-02.2,"Authority to Process Personally Identifiable Information | Automation

Manage enforcement of the authorized processing of personally identifiable information using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation provides robust, automated mechanisms essential for enforcing authorized processing of personally identifiable information. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) integrates with enterprise identity providers, ensuring that only authorized personnel and services can access or manage the underlying infrastructure components that host PII. Network segmentation capabilities within NSX-T Data Center isolate workloads processing PII, restricting communication paths to only those explicitly permitted. Furthermore, vSAN encryption and vSphere VM encryption provide automated data protection at rest, while NSX-T offers encryption for data in transit, ensuring PII confidentiality. Comprehensive logging and auditing features capture all relevant activities, providing the necessary data for monitoring and verifying enforcement.

The organization is responsible for defining what constitutes personally identifiable information within their environment and establishing the specific policies and authorization rules for its processing. This includes configuring the VCF platform's automated mechanisms, such as RBAC roles, NSX-T firewall rules, and encryption policies, to align precisely with these organizational definitions and authorization requirements. Ongoing management of these policies and the response to any detected unauthorized processing events remain an organizational responsibility."
PT-3,"PRI-02.1, PRI-05.1","Personally Identifiable Information Processing Purposes
a. Identify and document the [Assignment: Assignment organization-defined purpose(s)] for processing personally identifiable information
b. Describe the purpose(s) in the public privacy notices and policies of the organization
c. Restrict the [Assignment: organization-defined processing] of personally identifiable information to only that which is compatible with the identified purpose(s)

d. Monitor changes in processing personally identifiable information and implement [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms] to ensure that any changes are made in accordance with [Assignment: organization-defined requirements].",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides the foundational technical capabilities necessary to enforce restrictions on personally identifiable information (PII) processing and to monitor changes effectively.

Its robust identity and access management (IAM) capabilities, coupled with network virtualization and segmentation provided by NSX, enable organizations to precisely control who can access PII and from where, thereby restricting processing to only that which is compatible with defined purposes. Furthermore, VCF's comprehensive logging, auditing, and monitoring features across its compute, storage, and networking components allow for continuous oversight of PII processing activities and detection of any configuration or access changes.

While the platform offers these critical enforcement and monitoring tools, the organization remains solely responsible for identifying and documenting the specific purposes for processing PII. This includes describing these purposes within public privacy notices and organizational policies. The customer must also define what constitutes ""compatible processing"" and establish the specific requirements and mechanisms for managing changes to PII processing, ensuring these are aligned with their internal policies and external regulatory obligations.
The VCF platform provides the secure infrastructure, but the overarching governance, policy definition, and procedural implementation are external to its scope."
PT-3(1),"DCH-22.2, PRI-11","Personally Identifiable Information Processing Purposes | Data Tagging

Attach data tags containing the following purposes to [Assignment: organization-defined elements of personally identifiable information]: [Assignment: organization-defined processing purposes].",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides the secure, scalable, and resilient infrastructure required to host and operate advanced data governance, data loss prevention (DLP), and application-level data classification solutions. These solutions, deployed within the VCF environment, are capable of identifying and attaching data tags to specific elements of personally identifiable information (PII). The underlying storage and compute resources provided by VCF ensure that these tagging mechanisms can operate effectively across the data landscape, supporting the association of PII with defined processing purposes.

The customer is responsible for defining the specific elements of personally identifiable information that require tagging, as well as establishing the organization-defined processing purposes for each. Furthermore, the customer must select, deploy, configure, and manage the specific data governance or application-level tools within the VCF environment that perform the actual data tagging and metadata management. This includes developing and enforcing the policies and procedures governing the application and maintenance of these data tags."
PT-3(2),"PRI-02.2, PRI-10.1","Personally Identifiable Information Processing Purposes | Automation

Track processing purposes of personally identifiable information using [Assignment: organization-defined automated mechanisms].",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides the foundational infrastructure necessary to host and operate automated mechanisms for tracking personally identifiable information (PII) processing purposes. Its robust logging and monitoring capabilities across compute, storage, and networking components generate comprehensive audit trails that can be integrated with external data governance and security information and event management (SIEM) solutions. Network virtualization with NSX-T enables granular micro-segmentation, allowing for the isolation and controlled flow of PII data within specific application environments, thereby supporting the enforcement and monitoring of defined processing purposes. Furthermore, the platform's API-driven architecture facilitates seamless integration with third-party data classification and governance tools designed to identify and track PII processing activities.

However, the explicit definition of PII processing purposes, the selection and deployment of the specific organization-defined automated mechanisms (such as data classification engines or data governance platforms), and the ongoing operationalization and interpretation of the tracking data remain the direct responsibility of the customer. The platform provides the secure and auditable environment, but it does not inherently understand or define the business context or purpose of PII processing within guest workloads."
PT-4,PRI-03,"Consent

Implement [Assignment: organization-defined tools or mechanisms] for individuals to consent to the processing of their personally identifiable information prior to its collection that facilitate individuals’ informed decision-making.",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PT-4(1),PRI-03.1,"Consent | Tailored Consent

Provide [Assignment: organization-defined mechanisms] to allow individuals to tailor processing permissions to selected elements of personally identifiable information.",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides comprehensive identity and access management (IAM) services, including granular role-based access control (RBAC) and attribute-based access control (ABAC) capabilities. These services are foundational for defining and enforcing permissions across various data granularities.

The platform further offers APIs and SDKs that facilitate the integration of custom consent management workflows within customer applications. This enables applications to present options to individuals for tailoring data processing permissions and to securely record those preferences.

Additionally, data classification and tagging features within the VCF ecosystem can be leveraged by applications to identify and manage specific elements of personally identifiable information, allowing for differentiated processing based on individual consent.

The customer is responsible for designing and implementing the specific user interface and application logic that presents tailoring options to individuals. This includes defining what constitutes ""selected elements of personally identifiable information"" within their unique application and data models.

Furthermore, the customer must integrate the VCF's IAM and data management capabilities into their applications to enforce the tailored permissions effectively, and establish the necessary policies and procedures for obtaining, recording, and respecting individual consent."
PT-4(2),PRI-03.2,"Consent | Just-in-time Consent

Present [Assignment: organization-defined consent mechanisms] to individuals at [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] and in conjunction with [Assignment: organization-defined personally identifiable information processing].",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides the secure and resilient infrastructure necessary for deploying and operating applications that manage individual consent. It ensures the underlying compute, storage, and network resources are available and protected, forming a robust foundation for customer-developed or third-party consent management solutions. This includes capabilities for secure access, data isolation, and operational continuity, which are critical for any system handling sensitive personally identifiable information and consent.

The direct implementation and management of just-in-time consent mechanisms, including their definition, presentation frequency, and integration with specific PII processing activities, are responsibilities that reside entirely with the customer. The customer must design, develop, or procure application-level solutions to present consent mechanisms, define the triggers for consent presentation based on PII processing, and establish the frequency of such presentations. VCF does not provide native application-level consent management features."
PT-4(3),PRI-03.4,"Consent | Revocation

Implement [Assignment: organization-defined tools or mechanisms] for individuals to revoke consent to the processing of their personally identifiable information.",Contributes,"VCF provides the secure, resilient, and scalable infrastructure required to host and operate the organization-defined tools or mechanisms for managing personally identifiable information (PII) and processing consent revocations. The platform ensures the underlying compute, storage, and network resources are available and protected, forming the essential foundation for any deployed consent management solution.

The organization is responsible for defining, selecting, and implementing the specific application-level tools, identity management integrations, or dedicated consent management platforms that enable individuals to revoke consent to PII processing. This includes deploying these solutions within the VCF environment, configuring them to meet specific regulatory requirements, and establishing the necessary policies and procedures to manage and respond to revocation requests."
PT-5,PRI-02,"Privacy Notice
Provide notice to individuals about the processing of personally identifiable information that:
a. Is available to individuals upon first interacting with an organization, and subsequently at [Assignment: organization-defined frequency]
b. Is clear and easy-to-understand, expressing information about personally identifiable information processing in plain language
c. Identifies the authority that authorizes the processing of personally identifiable information
d. Identifies the purposes for which personally identifiable information is to be processed
e. Includes [Assignment: organization-defined information].",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PT-5(1),PRI-03.2,"Privacy Notice | Just-in-time Notice

Present notice of personally identifiable information processing to individuals at a time and location where the individual provides personally identifiable information or in conjunction with a data action, or [Assignment: organization-defined frequency].",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PT-5(2),PRI-01.2,"Privacy Notice | Privacy Act Statements

Include Privacy Act statements on forms that collect information that will be maintained in a Privacy Act system of records, or provide Privacy Act statements on separate forms that can be retained by individuals.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation provides a robust and secure infrastructure foundation for applications and systems that may collect and maintain information subject to Privacy Act requirements. The platform ensures the underlying compute, storage, and network components operate within a hardened and auditable environment, supporting the secure processing and storage of data. This includes capabilities for access control, data encryption at rest and in transit, and comprehensive logging, which are essential for maintaining the integrity and confidentiality of any system of records.

The implementation and display of Privacy Act statements on forms, or the provision of separate retrievable statements, are responsibilities that reside with the customer. This requires the customer to define and enforce organizational policies and procedures for data collection, design application-level forms to include the necessary statements, and manage the content and distribution of these privacy notices. The platform does not directly manage application content, user interfaces, or the specific legal text required for compliance."
PT-6,PRI-02.4,"System of Records Notice
For systems that process information that will be maintained in a Privacy Act system of records:

a. Draft system of records notices in accordance with OMB guidance and submit new and significantly modified system of records notices to the OMB and appropriate congressional committees for advance review
b. Publish system of records notices in the Federal Register
c. Keep system of records notices accurate, up-to-date, and scoped in accordance with policy.",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PT-6(1),PRI-02.5,"System of Records Notice | Routine Uses

Review all routine uses published in the system of records notice at [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] to ensure continued accuracy, and to ensure that routine uses continue to be compatible with the purpose for which the information was collected.",Contributes,"The VCF platform establishes a secure and auditable foundation for hosting systems of records. Its robust logging and monitoring capabilities track access patterns and data flows within the virtualized infrastructure, providing objective evidence of data handling. This operational data can inform an organization's understanding of how information is being accessed and utilized, which is critical input for a comprehensive review of routine uses.

The platform does not, however, automate the semantic analysis of System of Records Notices (SORNs) or the legal and policy determination of whether routine uses are accurate or compatible with the original purpose of data collection. Organizations are responsible for defining the review frequency, establishing the policy and procedures for evaluating SORN content, and conducting the manual or semi-manual review process to ensure ongoing compliance with privacy regulations."
PT-6(2),PRI-02.6,"System of Records Notice | Exemption Rules

Review all Privacy Act exemptions claimed for the system of records at [Assignment: organization-defined frequency] to ensure they remain appropriate and necessary in accordance with law, that they have been promulgated as regulations, and that they are accurately described in the system of records notice.",Not Applicable,"End user activity or process, design decision, or activity out of scope."
PT-7,"PRI-05.4, PRI-05.7","Specific Categories of Personally Identifiable Information

Apply [Assignment: organization-defined processing conditions] for specific categories of personally identifiable information.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation provides a robust, secure, and auditable infrastructure foundation essential for hosting workloads that process personally identifiable information. The platform enables the creation of logically isolated network segments using NSX-T, ensuring that specific categories of PII can reside within dedicated, protected enclaves. Data at rest can be encrypted through vSAN encryption or VM encryption, and network traffic can be secured, thereby establishing a secure environment for data processing. Access to the underlying infrastructure components is controlled via granular role-based access controls, limiting administrative privileges to authorized personnel.

The organization is responsible for defining the specific categories of personally identifiable information and establishing the precise processing conditions applicable to each category. This includes implementing data classification schemes, configuring application-level access controls, defining data retention policies, and ensuring compliance with these conditions within the guest operating systems and applications.
VMware Cloud Foundation does not inherently identify PII or enforce application-specific data handling policies
column2: it provides the secure infrastructure upon which these organizational policies and controls are built and enforced."

column1: PT-7(1),PRI-05.7,"Specific Categories of Personally Identifiable Information | Social Security Numbers
When a system processes Social Security numbers:
(a) Eliminate unnecessary collection, maintenance, and use of Social Security numbers, and explore alternatives to their use as a personal identifier
(b) Do not deny any individual any right, benefit, or privilege provided by law because of such individual’s refusal to disclose his or her Social Security number

(c) Inform any individual who is asked to disclose his or her Social Security number whether that disclosure is mandatory or voluntary, by what statutory or other authority such number is solicited, and what uses will be made of it.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation establishes a robust and secure infrastructure environment, offering foundational capabilities that support the protection of sensitive data, including Social Security Numbers, when processed by applications deployed within the platform.

The platform provides encryption for data at rest through vSAN encryption and VM encryption, as well as data in transit via NSX-T network encryption, ensuring that SSNs are protected from unauthorized access at the infrastructure layer.

Granular access controls within vCenter Server and NSX-T further restrict administrative access to the underlying compute, storage, and network resources, contributing to a secure processing environment.

The direct implementation of policies to eliminate unnecessary collection, maintenance, and use of Social Security numbers, along with the exploration of alternative identifiers, remains an an application-level and organizational policy responsibility. VMware Cloud Foundation does not dictate application design or data collection practices.

Similarly, ensuring that individuals are not denied rights or benefits for refusing to disclose their Social Security number, and providing mandatory disclosures regarding the purpose and authority for SSN collection, are functions of the customer's applications, business processes, and legal compliance frameworks. These aspects require explicit configuration and enforcement within the applications and operational procedures built upon the VMware Cloud Foundation infrastructure."
PT-7(2),PRI-05.7,"Specific Categories of Personally Identifiable Information | First Amendment Information

Prohibit the processing of information describing how any individual exercises rights guaranteed by the First Amendment unless expressly authorized by statute or by the individual or unless pertinent to and within the scope of an authorized law enforcement activity.",Contributes,"The VCF platform provides robust capabilities for data classification and tagging, enabling the identification and categorization of specific categories of personally identifiable information, including data related to First Amendment activities. Integrated identity and access management (IAM) controls, combined with granular policy enforcement, ensure that processing of such sensitive information is strictly controlled. SA-3(1),"PRM-07, SEA-07.1, TDA-07","System Development Life Cycle | Manage Preproduction Environment

Protect system preproduction environments commensurate with risk throughout the system development life cycle for the system, system component, or system service.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation provides a robust, software-defined infrastructure that inherently supports the creation, isolation, and protection of preproduction environments. Through NSX-T, micro-segmentation and distributed firewalling capabilities ensure network isolation and granular access control within and between preproduction environments, commensurate with defined risk profiles. vSphere and vSAN encryption secure data at rest and in transit for workloads hosted within these environments, while role-based access control (RBAC) within SDDC Manager and vCenter Server restricts administrative access to the underlying infrastructure. These foundational security features ensure that the platform itself provides a secure base for hosting system components and services throughout their development lifecycle.

The customer is responsible for defining the specific risk posture for each preproduction environment, establishing and enforcing security policies for applications and data residing within these environments, and implementing secure development lifecycle processes. This includes managing the sanitization and protection of test data, configuring application-level security controls, and ensuring that the overall SDLC integrates security practices for all stages of development, testing, and staging."
SA-3(2),TDA-10,"System Development Life Cycle | Use of Live or Operational Data
(a) Approve, document, and control the use of live data in preproduction environments for the system, system component, or system service

(b) Protect preproduction environments for the system, system component, or system service at the same impact or classification level as any live data in use within the preproduction environments.",Contributes,"VCF provides the foundational infrastructure to establish and secure preproduction environments. The platform offers comprehensive capabilities for network segmentation through NSX-T, enabling isolation of preproduction environments from production and other networks. Access controls can be rigorously enforced at the hypervisor, virtual machine, and network levels using vCenter and NSX-T policies. Data-at-rest encryption is available via vSAN encryption and VM encryption, ensuring that any data residing within these environments is protected according to its classification. Furthermore, VCF integrates with logging and monitoring solutions like vRealize Log Insight and vRealize Operations, providing visibility into activities within preproduction environments to detect and respond to unauthorized access or anomalous behavior. These technical controls allow for the protection of preproduction environments at a level commensurate with the impact or classification of any live data present.

The customer is responsible for establishing and enforcing the organizational policies and procedures governing the approval, documentation, and controlled use of live data within preproduction environments. This includes defining data classification schemes, implementing formal approval workflows for data transfers, maintaining comprehensive documentation of data usage, and ensuring that personnel adhere to these established controls. While VCF provides the technical mechanisms for protection, the overarching governance, policy enforcement, and human accountability for data handling remain with the customer."
SA-3(3),"SEA-07.1, SEA-08.1","System Development Life Cycle | Technology Refresh

Plan for and implement a technology refresh schedule for the system throughout the system development life cycle.",Contributes,"VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) provides robust lifecycle management capabilities that directly implement technology refresh for the SDDC stack. The platform automates the patching, updating, and upgrading of core components including ESXi, vCenter Server, NSX, vSAN, and SDDC Manager. This ensures that the underlying software infrastructure remains current, secure, and performs optimally throughout its operational lifespan. VCF's adherence to the VMware Compatibility Guide (VCG) and Hardware Compatibility List (HCL) also ensures that the deployed hardware remains supported and compatible with the software stack, providing a foundation for informed hardware refresh decisions.

While VCF automates the software refresh process for its managed components, the overarching technology refresh schedule for the entire system, including the underlying physical hardware, external storage, and any non-VCF managed network devices, remains a customer responsibility. Customers must define and document the comprehensive refresh policy, establish the specific refresh intervals for all system components, and manage the procurement and physical deployment aspects of hardware refreshes. The formal planning and scheduling of these activities, along with the associated budgetary and resource allocations, are outside the direct scope of VCF's automated lifecycle management."
